audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Jacob's embalming/mourning, Machpelah burial, brothers' fear, Joseph's providence statement, forgiveness, Joseph's death, and oath concerning bones.

verses.restored: Joseph fell on his father’s face, wept over him, and kissed him.
Joseph commanded his servants the physicians to embalm his father, and the physicians embalmed Israel.
Forty days were fulfilled for him, for so are the days fulfilled for embalming, and Egypt wept for him seventy days.
When the days of weeping for him had passed, Joseph spoke to Pharaoh’s house, saying, If now I have found favor in your eyes, please speak in Pharaoh’s ears.

My father made me swear, saying, Look, I am dying; in my burial place that I dug for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me. Now please let me go up and bury my father, and I will return.
Pharaoh said, Go up and bury your father, as he made you swear.
Joseph went up to bury his father, and with him went all Pharaoh’s servants, the elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt.
All Joseph’s house, his brothers, and his father’s house went up; only their little ones, flocks, and herds they left in Goshen.
Both chariots and horsemen went up with him; it was a very heavy camp.
They came to the threshing floor of Atad beyond the Jordan, and there they mourned with a very great and heavy mourning; he made mourning for his father seven days.
The Canaanite inhabitants of the land saw the mourning at the threshing floor of Atad and said, This is a heavy mourning for Egypt. Therefore its name was called Abel-mizraim, beyond the Jordan.
His sons did for him just as he had commanded them.
His sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham had bought with the field as a burial possession from Ephron the Hittite, before Mamre.
After burying his father, Joseph returned to Egypt, he and his brothers and all who had gone up with him to bury his father.
Joseph’s brothers saw that their father was dead and said, Perhaps Joseph will hate us and fully repay us all the evil that we did to him.
They sent a message to Joseph, saying, Your father commanded before his death, saying,

Thus you shall say to Joseph: Please forgive the transgression of your brothers and their sin, for they did evil to you. Now please forgive the transgression of the servants of the Divine of your father. Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
His brothers also went and fell before him and said, Look, we are your servants.
Joseph said to them, Do not fear, for am I in the place of the Divine?
You planned evil against me; the Divine planned it for good, in order to do as this day, to keep many people alive.
Now do not fear. I myself will sustain you and your little ones. He comforted them and spoke to their heart.
Joseph dwelled in Egypt, he and his father’s house, and Joseph lived one hundred ten years.
Joseph saw Ephraim’s children to the third generation; also the children of Machir son of Manasseh were born on Joseph’s knees.
Joseph said to his brothers, I am dying, but the Divine will surely visit you and bring you up from this land to the land he swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
Joseph made the sons of Israel swear, saying, The Divine will surely visit you, and you shall bring up my bones from here.
Joseph died, one hundred ten years old. They embalmed him, and he was placed in a coffin in Egypt.
